page: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage includes a 945 GB disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, with Geekbench single-core scoring 2121.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 31.34 GB/sec. Text processing is also strong at 2697.0. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall into the average range, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance at 15971.68 and large-block memory read bandwidth at 123.39 GB/sec. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which assist in workloads like small-scale LLM inference, where text generation for a 135M model achieved a strong 302.3 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16ds_v6 offers a high-density memory profile paired with dedicated compute resources and local storage, avoiding the premium of GPU-accelerated hardware. This makes it a balanced choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel GPU acceleration. The instance is appropriate for relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data processing pipelines, and enterprise applications requiring high memory capacity alongside fast local temporary storage.
description: The Microsoft Azure E16ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio. The instance includes 945 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2121.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.34 GB/sec, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. Supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, the E16ds_v6 is configured for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, in-memory caching, and data processing tasks that benefit from dedicated compute resources and fast local temporary storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ds_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local storage for demanding database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core performance in compression and text processing benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Optimized for databases, in-memory caching, and data processing workloads
